title: A Wish.
content:
Mine be a cot beside the hill,
    A bee-hive's hum shall sooth my ear;
    A willowy brook, that turns a mill,
    With many a fall shall linger near.

    The swallow, oft, beneath my thatch,
    Shall twitter from her clay-built nest;
    Oft shall the pilgrim lift the latch,
    And share my meal, a welcome guest.

    Around my ivy'd porch shall spring
    Each fragrant flower that drinks the dew;
    And Lucy, at her wheel, shall sing
    In russet gown and apron blue.

    The village-church, among the trees,
    Where first our marriage-vows were giv'n,
    With merry peals shall swell the breeze,
    And point with taper spire to heav'n.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Samuel Rogers, titled "A Wish.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
